title: Other Fees
rows: Gross sales includes all revenue related to franchised business, excludes sales/use tax, approved discounts/refunds. Non-traditional: 6%; lower rates set in unusual situations. If alcohol served and law prohibits royalties on alcohol, royalty increased proportionally.
Currently 4.50% of gross sales for franchisees; 1.75% for licensees
Company may propose increase up to 6%, requires majority vote approval.
Typically paid by franchisees to third parties. Company has not created cooperatives. If cooperative formed, franchisees may be required to pay. We reserve right to audit.
Only if franchisee subleases from Company.
Technology Support Project Management and Database Configuration
POS and ordering platforms, payment systems, inventory/labor management.
POS software/hardware maintenance, inventory/labor mgmt, menu management, network equipment rental, other approved services.
$299 to $1,600 per restaurant per project + project costs
Major projects classified by CTO. Includes project management, data collection, QA testing, vendor management, software deployment.
$250 per restaurant per period plus 0.21% of weekly gross sales
Flat fees each period (13/year); variable weekly
Help desk, menu management, platform services, cybersecurity, network config, QA, data hosting. We can modify this fee at any time.
Fees payable to vendor that administers Q-Cash program.
Olo Activation Fee ($250) + Fourth Implementation Fee ($299).
Begins 10 days after billing; due on demand
Subject to state law maximums. Charged on late Royalty, Marketing Fees, other fees.
Cost of audit plus 18% on unpaid amounts
Payable for follow-up audit, or if audit shows understatement/underpayment of at least 2% of gross sales for any month.
Payable on franchise sale or consent to restructuring.
Earlier of 30 days before expiration or at signing
For additional franchise term at same site under new Franchise Agreement.
New site must be in Protected Territory; open within 5 days of closing existing restaurant.
Currently $500 per visit plus actual expenses
No fee for first two visits. Third+ visits require fee plus travel/lodging/meals.
No fee for standard training content. All wages, travel, living expenses for trainees are franchisee responsibility.
Only for training beyond DO, GM, and two leaders/managers, or for excess training.
Up to $1,600 per trainee per program
DO, GM, and/or other leaders must attend as reasonably required.
Not incurred for first two franchised restaurants. Covers 11 days of training/opening support for 3rd+ restaurants.
Payable if restaurant fails food safety or operational inspection.
If approved suppliers can produce product but franchisee wants additional supplier approved.
If franchisee fails to correct deficiency, company may correct and obtain reimbursement.
Must reimburse Qdoba for claims resulting from restaurant operation.
Attorneys' fees due to failure to comply with Franchise Agreement.
Taxes on any above fees as required by taxing authorities.
Pro rata share of overall costs
Must participate in catering program; rewards program is part of it.
Greater of $500 or actual attorneys' fees and admin expenses
Due if franchisee requests or fails to comply with agreement and we prepare amendment.
Reimburse for EPR fees incurred due to franchisee restaurant operation.
Allocation of vendor cost multiplied by proportion of franchisee orders to total system orders.
table_notes: (1) If audit discloses franchisee failed to spend required local advertising amount, Company may directly debit bank account and contribute to Marketing Fund. Must also reimburse audit cost.

Fees are uniform except Company may in certain limited circumstances modify or waive. Unless otherwise noted, fees paid are not refundable. Certain fees may not apply to non-traditional restaurants. Non-traditional restaurants will not pay technology fees if they do not use Company's technology systems.

title: Your Estimated Initial Investment — Traditional Qdoba Restaurant
rows: Development costs: plans, legal fees, permits
Travel and living expenses while training
table_notes: TOTAL ESTIMATED COST (excluding real property and liquor license costs): $548,100 - $1,294,000

(1) No portion of initial investment paid to Qdoba is refundable. Based on 1,500-3,000 sq ft. Qdoba does not finance. Restaurant may be eligible for $100,000 cash incentive but estimate does not account for it.
(2) Franchise Fee is for one franchise. Different fee may be charged for non-traditional sites.
(3) Development costs vary depending on services and professionals selected.
(4) Leasehold improvements assume space includes landlord work letter requirements. AS-IS spaces will be higher.
(5) Must lease/purchase signs, kitchen equipment, interior decor, decorations.
(6) Systems include phones, POS, payment terminals, KDS, printers, network, training devices, security, safe, Q-card, music, digital menu boards, drive thru systems if applicable.
(7) Recipe items, opening food inventory, uniforms, smallwares, first aid, office supplies, cleaning supplies, gift cards, POP, opening cash drawer.
(8) Travel costs vary widely based on personal preferences; no estimate provided.
(9) $10,000-$25,000 required for grand opening (Franchise Agreement); License Agreement has no minimum.
(10) General liability and property only. Excludes builder's risk, workers comp, cyber, auto, earthquake/flood, other.
(11) Liquor licensing costs vary widely.
(12) Cost varies by location, market conditions, negotiation ability.
(13) Additional funds depend on time to achieve cash flow. Minimum recommended for 3-month contingency.
(14) Compiled from actual and expected costs in Company markets across US.

title: Chart 3: Historical Average Sales, Selected Costs and Operating Results (397 Franchised Restaurants)
table_notes: (1) Net Restaurant Sales = food, beverage, catering sales; excludes sales/service taxes less promotion costs (coupons, discounts, manager-complimentary items).
(2) Restaurant Cost of Sales section excludes depreciation and amortization.
(3) Cost of Sales includes food, paper, packaging, net of vendor rebates. May vary by geography and distributor.
(4) Salaries/benefits includes management wages (not above-store level), employee wages, shift supervisor wages, bonuses, payroll taxes, group insurance, workers comp, vacation, other benefits.

(5) Other operating includes repairs/maintenance, smallwares, cleaning, office, POS maintenance, pest control, delivery charges, credit card fees, bank charges, telephone/internet, trash, equipment rental, insurance, security, licenses/taxes, utilities, misc.

(6) Occupancy includes base rent, percentage rent, CAM, real estate taxes, personal property taxes, landlord-billed insurance, other lease expenses. Three franchisees had no reported lease (purchased real estate); 7.5% of sales imputed.
(7) Royalty is as defined in Item 6. Advertising Fees reflect combined 4.0% for FY ended 9/28/2025. Advertising Fees increased to combined 4.5% on 9/29/2025.
(8) Total Cost of Revenues does not include development fees, franchise fees, administrative costs.
Trailing twelve-month periods vary: 166 ending 9/28/2025, 118 ending 8/31/2025, 61 ending 8/3/2025, and smaller batches ending as early as 1/19/2025.
Excluded: 128 non-traditional, 5 refranchised, 38 newly opened, 13 Canadian, 7 closed 21+ days, 64 restaurants not yet loaded in portal.
Data is unaudited.
